titleStmt.title: Letter from John Robert O'Connell to Sir Matthew Nathan, 4 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Robert O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was written by Sir John Robert O'Connell to Sir Matthew Nathan, under-secretary for Ireland. O'Connell (1868-1943) was a solicitor applying for the chief crown solictior position. Nathan (1862-1939) was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 until 1916. In 1916 he faced criticism for failing to be aware of and to prevent the outbreak of the Easter Rising on 24 April. He resigned shortly afterwards. In this letter O'Connell is informing Nathan that he will be sending on a recommendation from John Mangan. Mangan (1853-1917) was the Bishop of Kerry in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/18

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
34, KILDARE STREET,
DUBLIN. 4th April 1916 Dear Sir Matthew Nathan I venture to trouble
you with another letter of
recommendation which
has been sent to me by
the Most RevdDr Mangan
Bishop of Kerry Yours Sincerely John Robt O'Connell
The Rt Honourable
Sir Matthew Nathan O.C. The Castle Dublin
